content.text: An ordered list of numbers arranged according to a definite rule. Each number in the list is called a term.
A sequence where consecutive terms differ by a fixed constant called the Common Difference (d).

content.name: AP Formulas
content.note: a = first term, d = common difference, l = last term, n = number of terms.

content.text: A sequence where each term is obtained by multiplying the previous term by a fixed constant called the Common Ratio (r).

content.name: Quadratic Formula
content.note: Discriminant (D) = b² - 4ac. If D > 0: Real/Distinct; D = 0: Real/Equal; D < 0: Complex.

content.name: 2x2 Matrix Operations
content.formula: A = \begin{bmatrix} a & b \\ c & d \end{bmatrix} \\ |A| = ad - bc \\ A^{-1} = \frac{1}{|A|} \begin{bmatrix} d & -b \\ -c & a \end{bmatrix}
content.note: If |A| = 0, the matrix is Singular and has no inverse.
